original_ticket: Customer noticed two separate charges of $49 on the same day and asked for one to be reversed immediately, worried it would overdraft their account.
resolution: Confirmed the duplicate was caused by a failed-payment retry that succeeded twice. Reversed the extra charge and issued a refund to the original payment method within 2 business days.
outcome: Refund processed, customer confirmed funds returned.

original_ticket: Customer wanted to cancel their annual plan two months in and requested a full refund, saying the product didn't fit their team's workflow.
resolution: The 14-day refund window had passed, but approved a prorated refund as a one-time exception given how early in the plan lifecycle the request came.
outcome: Prorated refund issued, subscription cancelled same day.

original_ticket: Customer's account got locked after too many failed login attempts from a shared office IP, and they couldn't unlock it themselves.
resolution: Verified identity over email, manually unlocked the account, and suggested enabling single sign-on to avoid shared-password lockouts going forward.

original_ticket: Customer couldn't log in after multiple password reset attempts, said the reset email never arrived.
resolution: Found the reset emails were being caught by the customer's corporate spam filter. Manually triggered a reset from the admin panel and walked them through setting a new password over chat.
outcome: Customer regained access in the same session.

original_ticket: Customer's integration started throwing 500 errors on every call to the /contacts endpoint after a bulk import of 10,000 records.
resolution: Traced it to a timeout caused by a missing index during bulk lookups. Added the index and deployed a fix, then confirmed the endpoint handled the same bulk load without errors.
outcome: Endpoint stable, customer's integration resumed normal operation.

original_ticket: Customer reported that CSV exports were silently truncating at 1,000 rows with no error message, even though they had over 4,000 contacts.
resolution: Found a hardcoded pagination limit in the export job. Removed the cap and added a warning banner for exports over 10,000 rows.
outcome: Full exports confirmed working, fix shipped to all customers.

original_ticket: Customer asked whether the platform could send Slack notifications when a high-priority ticket comes in, since their team already lives in Slack.
resolution: Native Slack notifications weren't available yet, so set up a temporary webhook workaround to bridge ticket alerts into their Slack channel, and logged the request with product.
outcome: Workaround delivered same week, native integration filed as a roadmap item.

original_ticket: Customer accidentally bulk-deleted their entire contacts list while testing a new import script and had no local backup.
resolution: Restored the account from the previous night's automated backup, recovering all but a few hours of same-day changes. Recommended exporting a backup before running bulk scripts going forward.
outcome: Data restored within 1 hour, customer resumed work the same day.

original_ticket: Customer needed to immediately revoke access for a team member who had just left the company, and wasn't sure removing a seat would affect that person's assigned tickets.
resolution: Walked the customer through removing the seat from Account Settings > Team, reassigning that person's open tickets to a teammate before removal so nothing got orphaned.
outcome: Access revoked immediately, no tickets lost.
